Home / Services / Drainage Solutions

Drainage Solutions

Effective drainage solutions to protect your property from water damage and erosion.

Albuquerque's monsoon season can bring heavy rainfall and flash floods, making proper drainage essential for protecting your property. Rocky Mountain Excavating specializes in designing and installing effective drainage solutions for residential and commercial properties. We understand the unique challenges of Albuquerque's arid climate and the importance of managing stormwater runoff. Our team offers a variety of drainage solutions, including French drains, swales, culverts, and retention ponds. We carefully assess your property's topography, soil conditions, and drainage patterns to design a system that effectively manages water runoff and prevents water damage. We also provide maintenance and repair services for existing drainage systems. We prioritize quality and durability, using high-quality materials and proven construction techniques to ensure your drainage system provides long-lasting protection. With Rocky Mountain Excavating, you can rest assured that your property is protected from the damaging effects of water.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Site Assessment - We evaluate the property's topography, soil conditions, and drainage patterns.
  2. 02 Step 2: Design - We design a drainage system that effectively manages stormwater runoff.
  3. 03 Step 3: Installation - We install the drainage system using high-quality materials and proven techniques.
  4. 04 Step 4: Testing - We test the drainage system to ensure it is functioning properly.

Benefits

  • Protects property from water damage and erosion
  • Prevents flooding and standing water
  • Improves property value
  • Reduces the risk of foundation problems

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a French drain?

A French drain is a trench filled with gravel or rock that contains a perforated pipe to redirect surface water and groundwater away from a specific area.

How do I know if I need a drainage system?

Signs that you may need a drainage system include standing water, soggy soil, foundation cracks, and water damage in your basement.

How much does a drainage system cost?

The cost of a drainage system varies depending on the size and complexity of the project. We provide a free estimate after assessing your property.
